message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Fisherman's Market & Grill (La Quinta) (La Quinta)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 744 reviews (4.0 stars), Tripadvisor 401 reviews (4.2 stars, #11 of 127 La Quinta restaurants); Google count [UNVERIFIED] but likely 1000+ given multi-platform footprint
- Sentiment: mostly positive
- Longevity: Founded 2002 by Louis Pagano and Bill Mortz; ~24 years operating; ownership appears stable across three Coachella Valley locations (Palm Springs, La Quinta, Palm Desert)
- Credentials: ["Repeatedly cited as 'Best Seafood' and 'Best Fish Tacos' in Coachella Valley local press / readers' polls (specific publication and year [UNVERIFIED] - claims of '16 consecutive years #1' appear in marketing copy but original award source not confirmed)","Shanghai Reds (sister concept under same ownership) named perennial honoree in Desert Magazine 'Best of the Valley' competition per Coachella Valley Independent"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for 'best fish tacos Coachella Valley/Palm Desert/La Quinta' and 'best seafood Coachella Valley' lists. Does NOT consistently appear in 'fine dining La Quinta' (loses to LG's Prime, Cliffhouse, Mitch's on El Paseo) or 'date night Rancho Mirage' (loses to Oceana, Catalan, The Edge, Wally's). Casual seafood category-defender, not fine-dining contender.
- Site structured data: fishermans.com is a Squarespace/Owner-platform site with location pages, embedded maps (lat/lon present), and explicit human-readable hours per location, but NO visible JSON-LD schema.org Restaurant markup detected. No itemprop microdata, no menu schema, no aggregateRating markup. Hours and menus are readable as text but not machine-structured for agents.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

## Top Recommendation: Fisherman's Market & Grill

Fisherman's Market & Grill has been voted best fish tacos, best seafood restaurant, and best value in the Coachella Valley. The restaurant is located at 78575 Highway 101, Suite 100 in La Quinta.

Their lunch special, Baja fish tacos, has been described as probably the best, freshest fish in a taco. The fish tacos are great and the fish and chips are lightly battered.

The restaurant also offers other popular options: Blackened Mahi-Mahi Tacos filled with juicy blackened mahi-mahi, pico de gallo, and a spicy chipotle aioli. The seafood tacos and galley sandwiches are delicious.

## Additional Details

They have indoor and outdoor seating with live entertainment on Friday and Saturday. You can order Ahi Poke, Fish n' Chips, or Blackened Mahi-Mahi Tacos.

For reservations or orders, you can call (760) 777-1601 or order online for pick up via DoorDash.

Links: [{"title":"FISHERMAN’S MARKET & GRILL - Updated June 2026 - 1127 Photos & 1114 Reviews - 235 S Indian Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, California - Seafood - +17603271766 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/fishermans-market-and-grill-palm-springs"},{"title":"Fisherman's Market & Grill, Greater Palm Springs, CA - Reviews, Ratings, Tips and Why You Should Go – Wanderlog","url":"https://wanderlog.com/place/details/1205498/fishermans-market--grill"},{"title":"Fisherman's Market & Grill","url":"http://fishermanslandingbarandgrill.com/"},{"title":"FISHERMAN’S MARKET & GRILL - Updated May 2026 - 393 Photos & 522 Reviews - 44250 Town Center Way C-2, Palm Desert, California - Seafood - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/fishermans-market-and-grill-palm-desert-4"},{"title":"Fisherman's Market & Grill Gift Cards - Online & Printable - 44250 Town Center Way, Palm Desert, CA","url":"https://www.giftly.com/gift-card/fishermans-market-and-grill-palm-desert-4"},{"title":"Louis Pagano - CEO - Fisherman's Market & Grill | LinkedIn","url":"https://www.linkedin.com/in/louis-pagano-636a1741/"},{"title":"Fisherman’s Market & Grill Now at Acrisure Arena - Coachella Valley","url":"https://coachellavalley.com/fishermans-market-grill-now-at-acrisure-arena/"},{"title":"Fisherman’s Market & Grill Now at Acrisure Arena | Coachella Valley Times","url":"https://coachellavalleytimes.com/stories/639167407-fisherman-s-market-grill-now-at-acrisure-arena"},{"title":"Fisherman's Market & Grill | Best Seafood in California | Seafood near me","url":"https://fishermans.com/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Fisherman's Market Grill's founding history:

## Founding History

Fisherman's Market & Grill was founded in 1993 in Palm Desert, though some sources list the establishment as 1995. The restaurant was created by Louis Pagano and his friend Bill Mortz, a commercial fisherman based in San Pedro.

## The Story Behind the Founding

After Pagano retired and moved to Rancho Mirage in 1990, he was approached a few years later by Bill Mortz who had a vision to open a fresh seafood restaurant in the desert. About five years after opening the first location in Palm Desert, they found property in Palm Springs and opened a second restaurant plus a second bar.

## Background and Inspiration

Louis Pagano's upbringing in San Pedro and his father Luigi's work as a commercial fisherman who opened small restaurants in the mid-1940s provided him with keen insight into the seafood and restaurant business. The restaurant emphasizes fresh seafood sourced directly from San Pedro's fishing docks.

**Morgan's in the Desert** - The undisputed anchor of fine dining in La Quinta is Morgan's in the Desert, the flagship restaurant at the La Quinta Resort & Club. It has been operating since 1926. The setting features warm lighting, lush courtyard surroundings, and a commitment to locally sourced ingredients, with meticulous plating and a considered wine list.

**Lavender Bistro** - Lavender Bistro, at 78073 Calle Barcelona, features an award-winning patio strung with thousands of sparkling lights, softened by fresh flowers, animated by live music. The menu is organic New American with fresh-caught seafood, fine meats, pastas, and thoughtful vegetarian and vegan options.

**La Brasserie Bistro & Bar** - This sophisticated French bistro is family-owned and showcases the talents of acclaimed chefs Emmanuel Janin and Vincent Perraud, with a menu featuring a delightful fusion of classic French dishes and innovative California-inspired cuisine.

**Cork & Fork** - Cork & Fork is a name that serious diners in the Coachella Valley return to consistently.

**Additional Options** - Other notable options include Lola's At The Cove, Kitchen Ten Eleven, La Quinta Cliffhouse, DSRT CLUB, and Italica.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: Notable: Founding year is contested. The brief says "2002 by Louis Pagano and Bill Mortz." But sources indicate 1993 or 1995 in Palm Desert. Also the "fine dining La Quinta" results show Morgan's in the Desert, Lavender Bistro, La Brasserie, Cork & Fork — not LG's Prime/Cliffhouse/Mitch's as the brief claims. Let me verify a couple more.

content.text:
I have enough to verify the claims. Key findings:

1. **Yelp 744 reviews** — Confirmed (Yelp listing shows "744 Reviews")
2. **Tripadvisor 401 reviews, #11** — Confirmed (401 reviews, ranked #11 of 108 La Quinta restaurants; brief said #11 of 127, count off)
3. **Founding 2002** — CONTRADICTED. Sources consistently say 1993 or 1995 in Palm Desert (~31-33 years, not 24)
4. **Founders Louis Pagano and Bill Mortz** — Confirmed
5. **16 consecutive years** — Repeated widely in marketing copy across listings, but original publication source not independently confirmed
6. **Fine dining La Quinta competitors** — Partial contradiction. Brief names "LG's Prime, Cliffhouse, Mitch's on El Paseo" — but Mitch's on El Paseo is in Palm Desert, not La Quinta; actual La Quinta fine dining anchors per multiple sources are Morgan's in the Desert, Lavender Bistro, La Brasserie. Cliffhouse is correct.
7. **No JSON-LD/schema.org markup** — Confirmed via fetch
8. **Surfaces for fish tacos/seafood queries** — Confirmed

confirms: Yelp 744 reviews confirmed via Yelp listing title
Tripadvisor ranking #11 confirmed (though denominator is 108 La Quinta restaurants per Tripadvisor, not 127 as claimed)
Founders Louis Pagano and Bill Mortz confirmed (Pagano listed as CEO on LinkedIn; Mortz a San Pedro commercial fisherman)
Three Coachella Valley locations confirmed: Palm Springs (235 S Indian Canyon), La Quinta (78575 CA-111), Palm Desert (44250 Town Center Way) — plus a fourth at Acrisure Arena now operating
No JSON-LD schema.org Restaurant markup, no microdata itemprop, no aggregateRating, no menu schema on fishermans.com La Quinta page — confirmed via direct fetch
Hours and addresses are present as readable text but not machine-structured — confirmed
Surfaces reliably for 'best fish tacos Coachella Valley' and 'best seafood' queries — confirmed (top organic hit, voted best fish tacos/seafood/value)
Does NOT surface in 'fine dining La Quinta' results (Morgan's in the Desert, Lavender Bistro, La Brasserie, Cork & Fork dominate) — confirmed casual-seafood positioning
Sentiment mostly positive confirmed across Yelp/Tripadvisor/RestaurantGuru aggregations
contradicts: Founding year: brief says 'Founded 2002... ~24 years operating' — multiple independent sources (Acrisure Arena coverage, biographical writeups) state the company was founded in 1993 or 1995 in Palm Desert, making it ~31-33 years operating, not 24. The 2002 date appears to be wrong.

Fine dining La Quinta competitor set: brief names 'LG's Prime, Cliffhouse, Mitch's on El Paseo' — but Mitch's on El Paseo is located in Palm Desert (El Paseo), not La Quinta, and LG's Prime Steakhouse is in Palm Springs. The actual La Quinta fine-dining anchors per Tripadvisor/OpenTable/Palm Springs Life are Morgan's in the Desert, Lavender Bistro, La Brasserie Bistro & Bar, and Cork & Fork. Cliffhouse is correct.
Tripadvisor denominator: claim says '#11 of 127 La Quinta restaurants' — Tripadvisor lists 108 restaurants in La Quinta, not 127.
flagged_as_unverified: '16 consecutive years #1 seafood' — phrase appears in marketing copy across multiple aggregator listings (visitgreaterpalmsprings, Yelp summaries, restaurant sites) but the original publication, poll, or year-by-year award trail is not surfaced. Cannot independently verify the original award source.
Google review count of 1000+ — not independently checked

Shanghai Reds 'perennial honoree in Desert Magazine Best of the Valley' — Shanghai Reds is confirmed as a sister concept with coverage in Coachella Valley Weekly, but the specific Desert Magazine award trail was not verified in this pass
Specific publication/year for 'Best Seafood' / 'Best Fish Tacos' citations — flagged in original brief and remains unverified
input.notes: The structured-data assessment is solid and the visibility/category positioning (casual seafood defender, not fine dining) is correct. However, the entity-fact section has two clear errors: founding year (2002 vs. actual 1993-1995) and the misattributed La Quinta fine-dining competitor set (Mitch's is Palm Desert, LG's Prime is Palm Springs). Tripadvisor denominator is also off (108 vs 127). The '16 consecutive years' claim should remain flagged — it propagates through marketing copy without a verifiable original source. Overall conclusion that this is a casual seafood category-defender with weak structured data is well-supported.
